title: NASA announces SpaceX Crew-13 assignments for ISS Expedition 75 mission
summary: NASA assigned four astronauts to SpaceX Crew-13, launching no earlier than mid-September 2026 to the International Space Station for Expedition 75, featuring an international crew from NASA, CSA, and Roscosmos.
contents:
## NASA Reveals SpaceX Crew-13 Crew for ISS Expedition 75

### Mission Announcement
NASA announced the crew assignments for SpaceX Crew-13, scheduled to launch no earlier than mid-September 2026 to the International Space Station (ISS) for Expedition 75. The international crew demonstrates continued cooperation among space agencies.

### Crew Assignments
- **Jessica Watkins** (NASA) — Spacecraft Commander
- **Luke Delaney** (NASA) — Pilot
- **Joshua Kutryk** (Canadian Space Agency) — Mission Specialist
- **Sergey Teteryatnikov** (Roscosmos) — Mission Specialist

### Mission Details
The crew will travel aboard a SpaceX Crew Dragon spacecraft for a long-duration stay aboard the ISS. The mission supports ongoing research programs and station operations during Expedition 75.

### Additional Space Updates
- The STORIE (Storm Time O+ Ring current Imaging Evolution) instrument, a NASA Goddard/US Space Force collaboration, completed preparation at Kennedy Space Center's Space Station Processing Facility for an upcoming ISS installation
- NASA and its international partners adjusted the ISS flight schedule, realigning launch opportunities for several upcoming missions to optimize planning, logistics, and timing
